questions.question: Why did Griffin set fire to his landlord’s house?
questions.answer: Griffin set fire to the house to take revenge on his landlord.

questions.question: Why did Griffin become homeless after becoming invisible?
questions.answer: He had to remove his clothes to stay invisible and had no shelter.

questions.question: Why was winter dangerous for Griffin?
questions.answer: Winter was dangerous because he could not wear clothes to keep warm.

questions.question: What mistake did Griffin make in the departmental store?
questions.answer: He slept too long and was discovered when the store opened.

questions.question: Why did Griffin attack the shopkeeper at Drury Lane?
questions.answer: He attacked the shopkeeper to steal money and obtain a disguise.

questions.question: Why did Griffin choose Iping village?
questions.answer: He chose Iping for solitude and secrecy.

questions.question: Why did Mrs Hall excuse Griffin’s strange behaviour initially?
questions.answer: She excused him because he paid in advance and claimed to be a scientist.

questions.question: Why did Griffin pretend to expect a cheque?
questions.answer: He pretended to expect a cheque to hide his lack of money.

questions.question: What made the clergyman’s burglary mysterious?
questions.answer: The room appeared empty though money was stolen.

questions.question: Why did Mrs Hall think the room was haunted?
questions.answer: Furniture moved on its own without any visible person.

questions.question: Why did villagers suspect Griffin of theft?
questions.answer: He suddenly produced money after claiming to be penniless.

questions.question: What shocked people when Griffin removed his disguise?
questions.answer: They saw a man without a visible head.

questions.question: Why was Mr Jaffers unable to arrest Griffin?
questions.answer: Griffin became completely invisible and escaped.

questions.question: What does Griffin’s behaviour reveal about his character?
questions.answer: It reveals his selfish and lawless nature.

questions.question: Why does invisibility isolate Griffin?
questions.answer: It separates him from normal social interaction.

questions.question: What fear dominated the villagers’ response?
questions.answer: Fear of the unknown dominated their response.

questions.question: Why did Griffin misuse his discovery?
questions.answer: He lacked moral responsibility and self-control.

questions.question: What genre best describes the prose?
questions.answer: The prose belongs to science fiction.

questions.question: What does the title highlight?
questions.answer: It highlights the mysterious footprints without a visible person.

questions.question: Why does Griffin fail to gain sympathy?
questions.answer: His violent and criminal actions remove sympathy.

questions.question: What human weakness does Griffin represent?
questions.answer: He represents arrogance and misuse of power.

questions.question: What lesson does the prose convey about science?
questions.answer: Science must be guided by ethics.

questions.question: Why did Griffin avoid people?
questions.answer: He feared exposure of his invisibility.

questions.question: What social reaction does the story show?
questions.answer: Society reacts with fear to unexplained events.

questions.question: What makes Griffin a tragic figure?
questions.answer: His scientific genius is destroyed by moral failure.
